expectations.text: respects-scope: Journey is scoped to wishlist sharing and gift coordination only — does NOT include product browsing, cart, checkout, or general e-commerce flows
expectations.evidence: The journey focuses exclusively on sharing a wishlist, opening shared links, marking items as bought, coordinating across friends, owner reviewing activity, and archiving. It does not include product browsing, catalog search, cart, or checkout phases. The only mention of purchasing is 'proceeds to purchase via checkout' in Phase 4 step 2, which is a brief handoff reference, not a full checkout flow.

expectations.text: phase-count: Output contains between 3 and 6 phases
expectations.evidence: Output contains exactly 6 phases: Phase 1 (Share a Wishlist), Phase 2 (Open the Shared Wishlist), Phase 3 (Mark an Item as Bought), Phase 4 (Coordinate Across Multiple Friends), Phase 5 (Owner Reviews Sharing Activity), Phase 6 (Gift-Giving Complete).

expectations.text: multi-user: Journey addresses both the wishlist creator and the friend/recipient perspectives
expectations.evidence: The overview explicitly states 'There are two personas involved: the Wishlist Owner who creates and shares the list, and the Friend (gift-giver) who receives the shared link.' Phases 1 and 5 focus on the Owner perspective, Phases 2 and 3 focus on the Friend perspective, and Phases 4 and 6 address both.

expectations.text: has-happy-paths: Every phase has a 'Happy path' section with numbered steps
expectations.evidence: All 6 phases have a '**Happy path:**' section with numbered steps. Phase 1 has 5 steps, Phase 2 has 4, Phase 3 has 5, Phase 4 has 4, Phase 5 has 4, Phase 6 has 4.

expectations.text: has-exceptions: Every phase has an 'Exceptions' section
expectations.evidence: All 6 phases have an '**Exceptions:**' section. Phase 1 has 3 exceptions, Phase 2 has 3, Phase 3 has 3, Phase 4 has 2, Phase 5 has 3, Phase 6 has 2.

expectations.text: has-mermaid: Output contains a valid Mermaid diagram
expectations.evidence: Output contains a ```mermaid code block starting with 'graph TD' at line 114, with a comprehensive flowchart covering all phases from owner sharing through archival.

expectations.text: gift-coordination: Journey explicitly covers the 'mark as bought' deduplication mechanism
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 is entirely dedicated to 'Mark an Item as Bought' with the deduplication mechanism explicitly covered. Step 4: 'the product immediately shows a Bought badge visible to all friends.' Exception: 'Item was already marked as bought by another friend (race condition): App shows a message: Someone already claimed this item.' Phase 4 reinforces this: 'each sees a real-time view of which items are still available and which are already marked as bought.'

All exceptions provide resolution: empty wishlist -> 'prompt suggesting the owner add at least one product'; link revoked -> 'message explaining that this wishlist is no longer shared'; race condition -> 'Choose another gift? and returns the friend to the wishlist'; not logged in -> 'prompts the friend to log in...after authentication, the friend returns to the same product'; friend changes mind -> 'taps Undo...to release the item back'.
